My Story Arts in Motion Wellness
About Me

Where Art
Meets Well-being

I'm Kate Berk — a community builder, arts-in-health practitioner, and person-centered coach based in Cleveland, Ohio. In fourth grade, a public school outreach program called Minds in Motion transformed the way I saw myself and the world around me. It showed me how to listen to my body, set goals with intention, and turn that discipline into meaningful connection with others. As much as I loved to dance, I always knew that its impact was so much more expansive than I could comprehend.

"A dance outreach program changed my life in fourth grade, and shaped the commitment I now bring to every community I work with."

I earned my BS in Dance–Arts Administration from Butler University and spent the next decade as a Principal Dancer and Outreach Lead with Ohio Contemporary Ballet. During those years I performed domestically and internationally, served as Dancer Representative, and facilitated programs that have had strong well-being outcomes, like Moving Connections, a movement-based wellness initiative for 100+ older adults across four senior living communities, and WiggleWords, an early literacy-through-movement program reaching 300+ children in five public schools.

It is in facilitation that my artistic and relational work converge. Whether teaching a pre-professional ballet student to strengthen her technique, working with an older adult discovering movement for the first time, or coaching a group of community members connecting with each other over Zoom, I meet people exactly where they are. I work with dancers and non-dancers alike; the goal is never performance for its own sake, but rather the kind of embodied self-awareness and genuine connection that movement makes possible. I also choreograph around the Cleveland area for community-building projects, including the Creative Healthy Aging collaboration between Summa Health and Akron Civic Theater, where I've created performance opportunities for adult actors over 55.

I now pursue an MA in Arts in Medicine (Social Prescribing focus) at the University of Florida, where I serve as a Research Assistant at the Center for Arts in Medicine Interdisciplinary Lab and as a reviewer for Perspectives on Arts in Health Journal. I also recently completed the National Board of Health and Wellness Coach certification through the Community as Medicine program at Open Source Wellness. I hope to use everything I have learned and lived to help people, and the communities around them, thrive.

Outside of work, I love traveling, taking on new physical challenges, consuming chocolate, coffee, and vegetarian delicacies, and spending time with my husband and our dog. Most of all, I enjoy building meaningful connections. I genuinely love people!

Arts in Health Social Prescribing Community Navigation Dance Education Trauma-Informed Care Wellness Coaching Arts Administration Conversational Spanish
Education & Training

Building a Foundation

MA in Arts in Medicine

University of Florida · 2025–present

Social Prescribing focus. Research Assistant, Center for Arts in Medicine Interdisciplinary Lab under Jill Sonke. Reviewer, Perspectives on Arts in Health Journal. Scholarship recipient. Completed Fall Arts in Health Intensive.

Community as Medicine

Open Source Wellness · 2025

National Board of Health and Wellness Coach Training. Passed all Practical Skills Assessments well above required metrics. Completed 50+ coaching sessions.

BS in Dance–Arts Administration

Butler University · 2012–2015

Highest departmental GPA, cum laude. Blue Key, Sigma Rho Delta, Kappa Alpha Theta honors. Instructor at Butler Community Arts School and Health & Recreation Center.

Trauma-Informed Practice

UCLA Arts & Healing Initiative

Certified training in culturally responsive, trauma-informed frameworks, centering safety, equity, and client self-efficacy across arts-based care contexts.

QPR Certified

Suicide Prevention Training

Trained in Question, Persuade, Refer, a research-backed suicide prevention protocol for practitioners working in community-based wellness settings.

Teaching Artistry Certifications

Kadenze Academy

Introduction to Being a Teaching Artist · Teaching Artistry for Social Impact

Career

Experience & Impact

2025
Arts & Wellness Coach
The Arts & Wellness Program

Conduct outreach and onboarding with transition-aged youth enrolling in arts-based wellness supports. Complete intakes and assess needs related to mental health, access barriers, and social connection. Provide trauma-informed, strengths-based coaching via virtual platforms and coordinate referrals with community partners for culturally relevant, accessible engagement.

2025–Present
Founder & Facilitator
Ohio Social Prescribing Collaborative

Pioneered the first Ohio social prescribing working group by mobilizing a team of elite Cleveland institutions, including Cleveland Clinic, Holden Forests and Gardens, and Playhouse Square, through virtual symposiums. Dedicate 6+ hours per week to transforming the way residents connect with their communities through arts-based social prescribing. Also designed and built the collaborative's website: ohio-social-prescribing.netlify.app.

2020–Present
Dance & Wellness Coach; Instructor
Self-Employed · Arts in Motion Wellness LLC

Provide individualized and semi-private coaching for participants of all ages and abilities, including those managing Parkinson's disease, chronic pain, and cancer. Choreographer for Akron Civic Theater's Creative Healthy Aging initiative in collaboration with Summa Health, creating performance opportunities for 20+ adult actors over age 55. Deliver virtual and in-person sessions in YMCAs, community centers, schools, parks, and in-home settings.

2015–2025
Principal Dancer & Outreach Lead
Ohio Contemporary Ballet (formerly Verb Ballets)

Principal dancer and outreach lead with Ohio Contemporary Ballet for a decade, touring and performing both domestically and internationally. Led Moving Connections, movement-based wellness programs for 100+ older adults across four senior living communities, and WiggleWords, early literacy-through-movement for 300+ students across five public schools. Engaged in 15+ public speaking events for audiences of up to 500 people. Served as peer-elected Dancer Representative on the Board and acted as Spanish translator on two tours to Cuba with the Cleveland Foundation. Launched partnerships with Cleveland State University, Cleveland Clinic, Workshop of Wishes, and NASA. Established an HR committee that enacted policy changes promoting culturally aware practices, including allowing dancers to wear skin-tone tights and pointe shoes.

2024–2025
Community Engagement Specialist & Administrative Assistant
Fairmount Center for the Arts

Coordinated 25+ community-facing programs and outreach initiatives in partnership with libraries, schools, senior facilities, cultural festivals, and municipal partners. Served as point of contact for eight partner organizations, scheduled 10+ events, and maintained strong communications through CRM platforms and email correspondence with 500+ families annually.

2015–2023
Dance Company Director
Fairmount Dance Company

Orchestrated 20+ annual lecture demonstrations and performances serving children, families, and older adults. Coordinated auditions, rehearsals, performances, communications, volunteers, and costumes. During tenure, dance department enrollment grew from fewer than 30 to 150+ students. Used movement as a platform to explore challenging social topics including racism, classism, and identity.

Community

Connecting & Prescribing

Community engagement isn't a side project, it's the heart of my practice. From senior living communities to public schools to neighborhood block parties, I know that connection is core to community health.

WiggleWords

OCB Educational Outreach · 2016–2025

Facilitated an early literacy program integrating movement and storytelling for 300+ students across five Cleveland public schools, making learning joyful, embodied, and accessible for every child.

Moving Connections

Ohio Contemporary Ballet · 2016–2025

Led movement-based wellness programming for 100+ older adults across four senior living communities in Greater Cleveland, reducing isolation, improving mobility, and building genuine community through dance.

CSU Physical Therapy Partnership

Cleveland State University · 2018–2024

Coordinated a cross-sector collaboration between Ohio Contemporary Ballet and CSU's Physical Therapy Department, reducing barriers to care for dancers and creating meaningful clinical learning opportunities for PT students.

Ohio Social Prescribing Collaborative

Founder & Facilitator · 2025–Present

Pioneered Ohio's first social prescribing working group, mobilizing elite Cleveland institutions including Cleveland Clinic, Holden Forests and Gardens, and Playhouse Square through virtual symposiums, transforming how residents connect with their communities. Visit the collaborative →

School & Library Outreach

Fairmount Center for the Arts · 2015–2025

Delivered lecture demonstrations and arts education programming to schools and libraries throughout Greater Cleveland, bringing ballet, storytelling, and arts-in-health education into every kind of community space.

Trailside Neighborhood Engagement

Volunteer · 2022–Present

Plan block parties and new neighbor welcome packages that are free to the community thanks to the support of 7 local sponsors that I've worked to secure.

Workshop of Wishes

OCB Partnership · 2015–2019

Partnered with Workshop of Wishes to bring personalized dance experiences to children with critical illnesses, offering joy, dignity, and movement to kids and families during extraordinarily difficult times.

Teaching

In the Studio

With over a decade of teaching experience spanning ballet, contemporary, jazz, creative movement, and wellness-focused movement, I meet every student where they are. From pre-professional ballet students to older adults discovering movement for the first time — the studio is a place of growth, curiosity, and genuine connection.

01

Private & Semi-Private Instruction

Individualized coaching for all ages and abilities — ballet technique, pointe, contemporary, alignment, injury prevention, and pre-professional mentorship. Available virtually and in-person.

02

Group Classes & Workshops

Classes in ballet, pre-pointe, pointe, variations, contemporary, modern, jazz, creative movement, and stretch/conditioning, offered at studios, YMCAs, community centers, schools, parks, and virtually — distance is no barrier to great teaching.

03

Intensive & Festival Faculty

Guest faculty for summer intensives and festivals. Enrichment courses in nutrition, self-care, injury prevention, dance history, choreography, improvisation, and stage hair/makeup.

04

Wellness-Focused Movement

Specialized programming for older adults, individuals managing chronic conditions, and anyone seeking movement for mobility, alignment, and well-being rather than performance goals.

05

Community & Outreach Teaching

Arts-based educational programming in schools, senior centers, libraries, and community organizations, making movement culturally relevant, accessible, and genuinely joyful.

06

SEL Through Dance

Using movement as a vehicle for meaningful conversation, exploring identity, belonging, resilience, and community. Informed by trauma-aware pedagogy and Social Emotional Learning best practices.

LLC
Arts in Motion Wellness

Where Movement
Becomes Medicine

Arts in Motion Wellness is my private practice at the intersection of movement, arts, and community health. Whether you're an individual seeking joyful embodied wellness, a healthcare provider curious about social prescribing, or an organization looking to bring arts-based programming to your community, I'd love to connect.

Private & Small Group Lessons

Virtual and in-person movement instruction tailored to your goals, ballet technique, joyful movement for wellness, alignment work, or dance as a tool for emotional regulation and self-expression. All ages and abilities welcome.

Social Prescribing Consulting

Consulting for healthcare systems, community health organizations, and nonprofits interested in integrating arts-based social prescribing into their practice, drawing on graduate research, clinical training, and a decade of community partnership work.

Arts in Health Program Design

Collaborative design and facilitation of arts-in-health programming for hospitals, senior communities, schools, and nonprofits. Trauma-informed, evidence-informed, and built around your community's specific strengths and needs.

Interested in working together?

Whether you're an individual, a healthcare provider, or an organization, I'd love to hear what you're working on and explore what's possible together.
